metadata.title: Technical Project Lead (TPL) Memorandum: SE Reports SE0004606-SE0004610 — FasFil Cigarette Tubes Substantial Equivalence
metadata.note: Actual PDF content is an FDA Center for Tobacco Products Technical Project Lead Memorandum for roll-your-own cigarette tube SE reports, not a clinical pharmacology biosimilar guidance. Rules extracted reflect the actual document content and the regulatory standards applied in the SE determination.

rules.rule_text: A new tobacco product may be marketed under the substantial equivalence pathway only if the new product does not raise different questions of public health compared to a predicate tobacco product that was commercially marketed in the United States as of February 15, 2007.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Office of Compliance and Enforcement (OCE) completed reviews...to determine whether the applicant established that the predicate tobacco products are grandfathered products (i.e., were commercially marketed as of February 15, 2007). The OCE review concludes that the predicate tobacco products are eligible predicate tobacco products, as the applicant has established that the predicate tobacco products are grandfathered.

rules.rule_text: New tobacco products must be in compliance with the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act as required by section 905(j)(1)(A)(i) of the FD&C Act before substantial equivalence orders can be issued.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Office of Compliance and Enforcement (OCE) also completed reviews to determine whether the new tobacco products are in compliance with the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), as required by section 905(j)(1)(A)(i) of the FD&C Act. The OCE reviews dated May 15, 2014, conclude that the new tobacco products are in compliance with the FD&C Act.

rules.rule_text: The SE report must undergo administrative completeness review before scientific review; the SE report must be administratively complete before proceeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Administrative completeness reviews were completed as follows: SE0004606, SE0004607: by Laila Noory, M.P.H. on November 21, 2012, and Chellee Cephas on January 28, 2013... The final administrative completeness review concludes that these SE Reports are administratively complete.

rules.rule_text: A chemistry review must be conducted to evaluate whether the new tobacco product raises different questions of public health with regard to product composition, including adequate characterization of all ingredients.
rules.verbatim_quote: The final chemistry review concludes that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health with regard to product composition. The significant product composition issue identified during the scientific review of the SE Reports was inadequate information required to fully characterize all ingredients in the new and predicate tobacco products. In response to FDA's scientific A/I letter, the applicant provided the appropriate characterization of the ingredients and materials used in the new and predicate tobacco products.

rules.rule_text: The applicant must provide adequate information to fully characterize all ingredients and materials used in both the new and predicate tobacco products to satisfy the chemistry review.
rules.verbatim_quote: The significant product composition issue identified during the scientific review of the SE Reports was inadequate information required to fully characterize all ingredients in the new and predicate tobacco products. In response to FDA's scientific A/I letter, the applicant provided the appropriate characterization of the ingredients and materials used in the new and predicate tobacco products.

rules.rule_text: An engineering review must be conducted to determine whether the new tobacco product raises different questions of public health with regard to product design, requiring adequate information to fully characterize both new and predicate products.
rules.verbatim_quote: The final engineering review concludes that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health with regard to product design. The significant product design issue identified during the scientific review of the SE Reports was inadequate information required to fully characterize the new and predicate tobacco products. In response to FDA's scientific A/I letter, the applicant provided the appropriate information and data to fully characterize the new and predicate tobacco products.

rules.rule_text: A toxicology review must be conducted to evaluate whether the new tobacco product raises different questions of public health with regard to product toxicity, including assessment of chemical ingredients and their levels of exposure.
rules.verbatim_quote: The final toxicology review concludes that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health with regard to product toxicity...The review concluded that the low level of exposure to these chemicals would not cause the new products to raise different questions of public health. Therefore, the toxicology review concludes that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health.

rules.rule_text: An environmental assessment must be provided by the applicant for SE tobacco product reviews, and a Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) must be supported before SE order letters can be issued.
rules.verbatim_quote: An environmental assessment was provided by the applicant. A finding of no significant impact (FONSI) was signed by Kimberly Benson, Ph.D. on May 29, 2014. The FONSI was supported by an environmental assessment prepared by Ronald Edwards, M.S. dated May 28, 2014.

rules.rule_text: SE order letters can be issued only when: the predicate tobacco products are grandfathered, the new product is in compliance with the FD&C Act, all scientific reviews conclude no different questions of public health arise, and a finding of no significant environmental impact has been made.
rules.verbatim_quote: The new tobacco products are currently in compliance with the FD&C Act. In addition, all of the scientific reviews conclude that the differences between the new and corresponding predicate tobacco products are such that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health. I concur with these reviews and recommend that SE order letters be issued. In addition, order letters can be issued because FDA examined the environmental effects of finding the new tobacco products substantially equivalent and made a finding of no significant impact.

rules.rule_text: In response to FDA's scientific administrative/information (A/I) letter, applicants must provide complete and adequate scientific responses; incomplete responses that do not provide requested information or substantive justification may result in the SE report being placed on hold.
rules.source_section: Section 1.2, Regulatory Activity Related to This Memo
rules.verbatim_quote: Following our review of the original and amended SE Reports, we sent a scientific A/I letter to the applicant in December 2013. The applicant responded to the scientific A/I letter by amending their SE Reports.

rules.rule_text: When differences between new and predicate tobacco products exist in package quantity or packaging design, these differences must be evaluated to determine if they raise different questions of public health.
rules.source_section: Section 1.4, Key Differences Between New and Predicate Tobacco Products
rules.verbatim_quote: The new tobacco products have the following key difference compared to the corresponding predicate tobacco products: Package quantity decrease (SE0004606, SE0004607, SE0004609, SE0004610 only).
rules.exceptions: Difference in package design alone does not cause the new products to raise different questions of public health

rules.rule_text: When new and predicate tobacco products use identical sub-components (e.g., filters and tubes), and differences are limited to packaging design, the chemistry review may conclude that no different questions of public health are raised with regard to product composition.
rules.verbatim_quote: Based on the information provided by the applicant, identical sub-components of the filters and tubes are utilized in the new and corresponding predicate products in all six of the SE Reports. The difference in the package design does not cause the new products to raise different questions of public health.

rules.rule_text: The TPL memorandum must capture all administrative, compliance, and scientific reviews completed for SE reports and serve as the basis for the SE order recommendation.
rules.verbatim_quote: This memo captures all administrative, compliance, and scientific reviews completed for SE0004606-SE0004610.

rules.rule_text: Predicate tobacco products must be eligible predicate tobacco products, established by demonstrating the predicate products are grandfathered (commercially marketed in the United States as of February 15, 2007).
rules.verbatim_quote: The predicate tobacco products meet statutory requirements because they are grandfathered products (i.e., were commercially marketed in the United States as of February 15, 2007).

rules.rule_text: The differences between new and predicate tobacco products must be minor and inconsequential with regard to public health for a substantial equivalence determination to be made.
rules.verbatim_quote: The new and corresponding predicate products are essentially identical with the exception of the above differences. And, the differences that do exist are minor and inconsequential with regard to public health.

rules.rule_text: Scientific reviews for tobacco product SE reports must be completed by qualified reviewers across multiple disciplines including chemistry, engineering, and toxicology.
rules.verbatim_quote: Scientific reviews were completed by the Office of Science (OS) for the following disciplines: [Chemistry, Engineering, Toxicology]

rules.rule_text: When new tobacco products have the same product design as predicate products, the engineering review may conclude that no different questions of public health are raised with regard to product design.
rules.verbatim_quote: Based on the information and data provided by the applicant, the new and corresponding predicate products are identical in product design. Therefore, the final engineering review concludes that the new tobacco products do not raise different questions of public health with regard to product design.

rules.rule_text: Amendments to original SE Reports may be required in response to administrative advice and information request letters (A/I letters); multiple rounds of amendment may be necessary before SE review is completed.
rules.source_section: Section 1.2, Regulatory Activity Related to This Memo
rules.verbatim_quote: The applicant submitted the five original SE Reports listed in Table 2 of this memo. We sent the applicant administrative advice and information request letters (A/I letters) for these SE Reports. In response, the applicant submitted amendments to the original SE Reports.

rules.rule_text: For tobacco product SE reports, the Office of Compliance and Enforcement must complete a review to verify eligibility of the predicate product as a grandfathered tobacco product prior to granting SE status.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Office of Compliance and Enforcement (OCE) completed reviews for SE0004606, SE0004608, and SE0004610 on March 20, 2013, for SE0004607 on March 19, 2013, and for SE0004609 on May 30, 2013, to determine whether the applicant established that the predicate tobacco products are grandfathered products.

rules.rule_text: The Technical Project Lead (TPL) must concur with scientific reviews and recommend issuance of SE orders; a signatory decision from the Director of the Office of Science is required to finalize the SE order recommendation.
rules.verbatim_quote: I concur with these reviews and recommend that SE order letters be issued... SE order letters should be issued for the new tobacco products in SE0004606-SE0004610, as identified on the cover page of this memo.
